description: Quality rules for Adventure Capitalist-style idle clickers, tycoon games, and management sims (e.g. Tiny Tower). Covers tick pacing, hire/unlock gating, visual richness per business, satisfying feedback, and the offline-progress / prestige loop.

guidelines.rule: Passive / auto-actions (auto-elevator, auto-collect, manager ticks) MUST run on slow intervals - 30 to 60 seconds is the sweet spot. Fast intervals (every 1-5s) read as distracting noise, not ambient life.
guidelines.why: Direct user feedback on Tiny Tower v0001-v0004: 'the auto-elevator is ok but it's so active it's distracting. It should probably move every 45 seconds.' Idle games feel best when ambient activity is calm and the player's taps feel impactful by contrast.
examples: setInterval(autoElevatorTick, 45000)  // not 3000
Visitor spawns every 6-10s, not every 500ms
Manager auto-collect tick: 30-60s per business

guidelines.rule: Any passive timer MUST be tap-accelerable. Tapping the elevator, the collect button, or the spawner should immediately advance / fire the next tick.
guidelines.why: Slow ambient ticks (rule ig-001) only work if the impatient player has an outlet. Tap-to-accelerate is the contract: ambient by default, instant on demand.
examples: onclick on elevator: clearTimeout + autoElevatorTick() immediately
Tap a business once to collect immediately AND reset its timer
Visible tap target with a subtle hover/pressed state

guidelines.rule: Passive workers / managers / NPC roles (doorman, manager, security, cleaner) MUST be explicitly hired before they activate. Do not auto-spawn them as free background extras.
guidelines.why: Direct user feedback on Tiny Tower: 'I should have to hire the doorman I suppose before that gets activated.' The hire gate is the core meta-loop - every unlock should feel earned. Free passive roles trivialize early-game progression.
examples: state.doormanHired = false  // until purchased
Auto-elevator only runs once doorman is hired
Manager auto-collect locked behind a per-business hire button

guidelines.rule: Each business / floor / production cell MUST be visually rich - multiple distinct objects, animated NPCs, decor, signage. A floor with one centered label looks like a placeholder.
guidelines.why: Direct user feedback on Tiny Tower v0004-v0005: 'the floors are still very empty! I see a two or 3 things on either side of the screen but the whole space is not very well appointed.' The visual density of each business is what differentiates a polished tycoon from a spreadsheet.
examples: Each floor: signage + 3-5 furniture items + 1-3 animated bitizens/customers + ambient detail (plants, posters, products on shelves)
Vary furniture set per business type - bakery has ovens + display cases; cafe has tables + espresso machine
Reserve the empty-floor look ONLY for un-purchased / locked floors

guidelines.rule: Use an exponential cost curve for upgrades and new units. Standard formula: cost = base * multiplier^owned, where multiplier is 1.07-1.15.
guidelines.why: Idle game pacing collapses without exponential costs - players hit a wall too fast (multiplier too high) or never feel progression friction (linear costs). 1.15 is the Adventure Capitalist baseline; tune lower (1.07-1.10) for slower-pacing tycoons.
examples: nextCost = Math.ceil(baseCost * Math.pow(1.15, owned))
Display the formula's result on the button so the player sees the next step
Bulk-buy buttons (x10, x100) compute the sum of the geometric series

guidelines.rule: Every coin/income gain MUST produce immediate visual + (optional) audio feedback - a number that floats up and fades, a coin burst, a brief scale-pop on the counter. Silent income increments feel broken.
guidelines.why: Idle games are 80% game-feel. The dopamine loop IS the gameplay. A +$5 that just updates a counter is invisible; a +$5 that floats up in gold and the counter bounces is the entire reward.
examples: On collect: spawn floating '+$N' text that translateY(-40px) opacity 1->0 over 800ms
Counter scale: 1 -> 1.08 -> 1 over 150ms on each increment
Optional coin-clink WebAudio blip (gated by a sound toggle)

guidelines.rule: Persist game state on a short interval (5-10s) AND on every meaningful action (hire, purchase, upgrade, prestige). Use localStorage with a versioned schema key.
guidelines.why: Idle games are long-horizon. A player who closes the tab and loses an hour of progress will not return. Tiny Tower v0005 does setInterval(saveGame, 5000) - keep that pattern. Versioned key lets you migrate state shape later without nuking saves.
examples: localStorage.setItem('tinytower.save.v1', JSON.stringify(state))
setInterval(saveGame, 5000)
Also save inside hireBitizen(), buyUpgrade(), prestige() - not just on the timer

guidelines.rule: On load, calculate offline progress: elapsed_seconds = (now - lastSaveTimestamp), accrue passive income for that window (capped at e.g. 8h), and show a 'Welcome back - you earned $X while away' modal.
guidelines.why: Offline progress is the second core retention loop after the active tap loop. Players who reload and see nothing happened feel like the game stopped existing without them. Cap the window so the game can't be exploited by setting the system clock forward.
examples: const elapsed = Math.min(8*3600, (Date.now() - state.lastSaved) / 1000)
const earned = elapsed * passiveIncomeRate * offlineEfficiency  // efficiency 0.5-1.0
Welcome-back modal with [Collect] button; counter pops on collect

guidelines.rule: Provide a long-horizon prestige / reset loop: at a milestone (e.g. $1B total earned), the player can reset progress in exchange for a persistent multiplier (e.g. golden tickets / influence / VIPs).
guidelines.why: Without prestige, idle games end. The player hits the top business and quits. Prestige resets the short-loop (rebuy everything) while preserving the long-loop (the multiplier compounds across resets). This is the entire late-game.
examples: Unlock the prestige button at $1B lifetime earnings; show it grayed-out before
Prestige tokens = floor(sqrt(lifetimeEarnings / 1e9)); each token = +2% global income
Confirm modal: 'Reset all businesses and managers in exchange for N tokens?'

guidelines.rule: The main income counter MUST be the largest, most prominent element on screen, formatted with commas / suffixes (K, M, B, T) so 100,000,000 reads as '$100M' not '100000000'.
guidelines.why: The number going up IS the game. Cramped, unformatted counters look like debug output. K/M/B/T abbreviation handles late-game numbers that otherwise overflow their container at $1,000,000,000+.
examples: function fmt(n) { if(n<1e3)return n; if(n<1e6)return (n/1e3).toFixed(1)+'K'; if(n<1e9)return (n/1e6).toFixed(2)+'M'; if(n<1e12)return (n/1e9).toFixed(2)+'B'; return (n/1e12).toFixed(2)+'T'; }
Counter font-size >= 32px, weight 700+, monospaced or tabular-nums to prevent layout jitter
Position: top-center or top-bar, never tucked in a sidebar
